perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be a preliminary draft until all parties have signed identical copies, and only then shall all counterparts together be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an ambiguity regarding when the agreement becomes binding. Originally, each counterpart was considered an original. Now, they are considered preliminary drafts until everyone signs identical copies. This contradicts the implicit understanding that signing any counterpart binds a party, as it is unclear whether individual signed counterparts are independently enforceable before complete matching execution. This also affects the enforceability of the agreement because now to make the agreement valid we would need to proof identical copies signed by each party, but it is never specified what identical copies are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be a preliminary draft until all parties have signed identical copies, and only then shall all counterparts together be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be a preliminary draft until all parties have signed identical copies, and only then shall all counterparts together be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which the agreement is considered valid. Originally, each counterpart's execution was sufficient to create a binding agreement. The modification introduces a requirement that all parties sign identical copies for the agreement to be effective, which directly contradicts the initial understanding and creates uncertainty about the enforceability of individual counterparts prior to achieving identical execution by all parties. This is an 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because the terms are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement as of July 6, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned may execute this Agreement at their discretion, with the effective date to be determined by mutual consent of a majority of signatories, but no earlier than July 6, 2020.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the execution and effective date uncertain. Originally, signing was a declaration of execution as of a specific date. Now, execution is discretionary and the effective date depends on later agreement. This contradicts the immediate confirmation of agreement to joint filing stated earlier. If only one person signs on July 6, and the other after two months, what happens to the joint filing agreement during that time?
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agreement is executed 'as of July 6, 2020', indicating a fixed execution date. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating the agreement 'may be executed at their discretion, with the effective date to be determined by mutual consent', contradicting the original fixed date. This creates an in-text contradiction as it conflicts with the 'Contradicted Text', which confirms an immediate agreement to joint filing, since the new text introduces potential delays and disagreements on the effective date, leading to uncertainty about when the joint filing agreement becomes effective.

perturbation.original_text: ATHENE ANNUITY AND LIFE COMPANY By: Apollo Insurance Solutions Group LP, its investment adviser By: AISG GP Ltd., its general partner By: /s/ Matthew S. O'Mara Matthew S. O'Mara Authorized Signatory
perturbation.changed_text: ATHENE ANNUITY AND LIFE COMPANY By: Apollo Insurance Solutions Group LP, its investment adviser By: AISG GP Ltd., its general partner By: /s/ Matthew S. O'Mara Matthew S. O'Mara Potential Signatory
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the authority of the signatory. Changing 'Authorized Signatory' to 'Potential Signatory' creates uncertainty about whether the person signing has the power to bind the company. This contradicts the entire purpose of the agreement, which assumes each signatory has the necessary authorization to commit their respective entity to joint filing. If the person doesn't have the power to bind the company, it is unclear if the company is entering into the agreement or not
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Authorized Signatory' to 'Potential Signatory' introduces ambiguity regarding the signatory's authority. This directly contradicts the purpose of the joint filing agreement, which relies on each signatory having the authority to bind their respective entity. The contradicted text explicitly states the agreement is among entities 'to the joint filing on behalf of them,' implying authorized representation. The term 'Potential Signatory' undermines this assumption, creating a significant contradiction because it is unclear if the company is actually agreeing to the joint filing, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.
